sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson MedTech sales up 7.5% in Q4
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported strong fourth-quarter results, with sales beating analyst expectations and earnings matching them. The company's MedTech segment saw a 7.5% increase, driven by acquisitions and key product lines like electrophysiology and Abiomed heart pumps. J&J's CEO highlighted a "catapult year" fueled by innovation across its six key businesses and provided an optimistic outlook for 2026.

feed.title: Those Who Invested in MSCI (NYSE:MSCI) Five Years Ago Are up 52%
feed.summary: MSCI Inc. (NYSE:MSCI) shareholders have seen a 44% share price increase over the last five years, which is less than the broader market return, though the total shareholder return (TSR) including dividends reaches 52%. The company's compounded earnings per share (EPS) growth was 19% per year over the same period, suggesting the market has become somewhat pessimistic. Despite a 5.4% share price drop in the last year, insider buying has been noted, and the article suggests reviewing fundamental data for long-term growth trends.

feed.title: EQT stock rating reiterated at Buy by UBS ahead of Q4 earnings
feed.summary: UBS has reiterated its Buy rating and $76.00 price target for EQT Corp. (NYSE:EQT) ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings report. Despite recent share price declines due to weather and accelerated infrastructure spending, UBS maintains a positive outlook, citing EQT's low price volatility, expected reduction in net debt below target, and strong long-term business fundamentals. The company has also been active with diverse investments and divestitures in other sectors.

feed.title: Apollo, Ares, Blackstone Partner With OneDigital To Bring Private Markets To 401ks
feed.summary: OneDigital has partnered with global asset managers Apollo, Ares, and Blackstone to introduce private investments into its personalized portfolio program for 401k plan sponsors. This initiative aims to expand access to institutional-quality private equity, private credit, and opportunistic investment strategies for eligible plan sponsors, leveraging a fiduciary-led approach to potentially improve retirement outcomes by offering diversification and reduced volatility.

feed.title: Prologis posts Q4 revenue beat on strong leasing; issues 2026 guide (PLD:NYSE)
feed.summary: Prologis reported better-than-forecast Q4 2025 sales driven by strong lease signings, meeting Wall Street expectations for earnings and providing full-year 2026 guidance. The logistics real estate company anticipates its FY26 core FFO per share to align with analyst projections.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway: Exiting Kraft Heinz Could Be First Move to Pare Down Equity Investment Portfolio
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway is reportedly selling its entire stake in Kraft Heinz, a move stemming from Warren Buffett's and incoming CEO Greg Abel's dissatisfaction with the company's decisions. This sale, which could result in a $1.3 billion realized loss for Berkshire, is seen as Abel's initial step to streamline the firm's investment portfolio. Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate for Berkshire Hathaway, viewing the stock as slightly undervalued despite this development.

feed.title: Barclays Has Lowered Expectations for EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) Stock Price
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) from $136 to $133, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, though this still implies a 22.66% upside. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with a target of $136.89, despite recent price target cuts from other firms like UBS, BMO, and Goldman Sachs. EOG Resources recently beat its quarterly EPS estimates but saw revenue decline, and insider selling has been noted while institutional ownership remains high.

feed.title: Barclays Boosts Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) Price Target to $42.00
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) from $40.00 to $42.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, which suggests an 11% upside from its previous close. Devon Energy's stock saw a 4.2% jump to $37.83 following the update, with a market cap of $23.7 billion and a P/E ratio of 8.92. The company reported quarterly EPS of $1.04, surpassing analyst estimates, and currently holds a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating among analysts with an average price target of $44.38.

feed.title: Danaher (NYSE:DHR) Stock Price Expected to Rise, The Goldman Sachs Group Analyst Says
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group has raised its price target for Danaher (NYSE:DHR) to $270 from $265, maintaining a "buy" rating and indicating a potential upside of 13.63%. This follows Danaher's recent earnings beat, with reported EPS of $1.89 against an estimated $1.72, and revenue of $6.05 billion. Despite some insider selling, institutional investors hold a significant 79.05% stake in the company, which has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts.

feed.title: Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) Given New $74.00 Price Target at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) to $74.00 from $70.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, indicating a potential upside of 4.54%. This new target contributes to a broader "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, with a collective price target of $79.47. Corteva recently surpassed quarterly earnings estimates, reporting -$0.23 EPS and $2.62 billion in revenue, exceeding expectations.

feed.title: Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) Given New $75.00 Price Target at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has increased its price target for Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) to $75.00 from $70.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 5.95% from the current stock price. Corteva also recently reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, leading to a 2.7% rise in its share price.

feed.title: Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) Given New $34.00 Price Target at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) from $35.00 to $34.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. This new target implies a potential upside of 26.71% from the stock's previous closing price. The consensus among analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" for CTRA, with an average price target of $32.57.

feed.title: CF Industries (NYSE:CF) Given New $90.00 Price Target at BNP Paribas Exane
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has raised its price target for CF Industries (NYSE:CF) from $80.00 to $90.00 while maintaining a "neutral" rating, indicating a potential upside of 0.59% from the current stock price. Despite this increase, the analyst consensus remains a "Hold" with an average target of $92.63, reflecting cautious sentiment. CF Industries recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates and holds a market capitalization of $13.96 billion with significant institutional ownership.

feed.title: Scotiabank Cuts Republic Services (NYSE:RSG) Price Target to $242.00
feed.summary: Scotiabank has reduced its price target for Republic Services (NYSE:RSG) to $242.00 from $246.00, while maintaining a "sector perform" rating. This new target implies a potential 13.9% upside from the current stock price. Despite this, the consensus among analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $248.86, and the company recently reported stronger-than-expected EPS.

feed.title: Bank of America Increases ON Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) Price Target to $65.00
feed.summary: Bank of America has raised its price target for ON Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) to $65.00 from $60.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, indicating a potential upside of nearly 5%. This comes as ON Semiconductor authorized a $6.0 billion share buyback program, suggesting management views the stock as undervalued. Despite a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a $61.22 price target from analysts, the company's Q3 results showed an EPS beat but a revenue decline year-over-year, alongside a high P/E ratio.

feed.title: CACI International Likely To Report Higher Q4 Earnings; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
feed.summary: CACI International (NYSE: CACI) is expected to announce higher Q4 earnings, with analysts forecasting $6.49 per share on $2.28 billion in revenue, up from the previous year. Ahead of the earnings call on Wednesday, January 21, several analysts have revised their price targets for CACI, with a mix of maintained Buy and Neutral ratings. The company recently secured a $416 million task order from the U.S. Navy.

feed.title: Nucor to Report Q4 Earnings: What's in the Offing for the Stock?
feed.summary: Nucor (NUE) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings after the closing bell on January 26, 2026. The company is expected to benefit from a recovery in U.S. steel prices during the quarter, despite facing headwinds from seasonal factors, lower shipment volumes across its operating segments, and planned outages. Analysts anticipate a year-over-year revenue increase of 9.8%, but Nucor's Earnings ESP suggests a potential miss on earnings expectations.

feed.title: Eye on Processing: Regions Bank Picks Worldpay; CU Switches to Velera
feed.summary: Regions Bank has selected Worldpay, a unit of Global Payments Inc., to provide payment processing services for its Regions Merchant Services business, offering various in-store and online payment options, including mobile contactless acceptance and fraud protection. Concurrently, Velera announced that Financial Center First Credit Union will be adopting its debit and ATM processing services in July, followed by credit card transactions in August. These moves highlight ongoing shifts in financial institutions' choices for payment processing partners to enhance their offerings to customers.

feed.title: What to Expect From Monster Beverage's Next Quarterly Earnings Report
feed.summary: Monster Beverage Corporation (MNST) is expected to announce its fiscal Q4 2025 earnings soon, with analysts forecasting a 26.3% rise in profit to $0.48 per share. The company has a strong track record of beating earnings estimates and delivered record revenue in Q3 due to solid demand for its energy drinks. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for MNST, with potential upside indicated by the Street-high price target.

feed.title: UnitedHealth plans to rebate exchange profits to customers
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group announced its intention to rebate exchange profits to customers. This comes amidst other healthcare industry news including Blue Cross plans testing patient willingness to shop for healthcare, health insurance CEOs facing grilling on Capitol Hill, and discussions around cybersecurity breaches and revenue cycle demands in health tech.

feed.title: Park Place Kroger Marketplace opening in June; South Charleston, Dunbar locations closing
feed.summary: The first Kroger Marketplace in West Virginia is set to open in South Charleston's Park Place in early June, leading to the closure of the South Charleston Riverwalk Mall and Dunbar Village stores. This new $40 million Kroger Marketplace will be the largest in the state, spanning 122,000 square feet and featuring a full-service grocery, fuel center, pharmacy, home goods, apparel, and toys. Affected employees from the closing stores will be offered positions at nearby Kroger locations.

feed.title: Darden restaurants’ Wilkerson sells $480k in shares
feed.summary: John W. Wilkerson, President Elect of Olive Garden at Darden Restaurants Inc, sold 2,258 shares of common stock for $480,954 and gifted 670 shares for charitable contributions on January 16, 2026. This transaction occurred with DRI shares trading near their 52-week high, and InvestingPro analysis indicates the stock is trading above its Fair Value and is in overbought territory. Darden Restaurants, with a $23.8 billion market cap, has consistently paid dividends for 32 years and has a P/E ratio of 21.7.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts DTE Energy Price Target to $142 From $138, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for DTE Energy (DTE) to $142 from $138, while reaffirming an Overweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects the firm's continued positive outlook on the energy company. The article also lists other recent analyst adjustments for DTE Energy, with various firms maintaining or slightly adjusting their price targets and ratings.

feed.title: Fastenal Annual Sales Hit $8.2B, though Margin Dips in 4Q
feed.summary: Fastenal achieved a new annual revenue record of $8.2 billion in 2025, driven by increased sales effectiveness and pricing actions, despite sluggish market conditions. The company's fourth-quarter sales grew by 11.1% year-over-year, but gross margin experienced a slight dip. This marked Fastenal's sixth consecutive month of double-digit daily sales growth.

feed.title: FCX vs. BHP: Which Copper Mining Giant Should You Invest in Now?
feed.summary: This article compares two major copper mining companies, Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX) and BHP Group Limited (BHP), to determine which offers a better investment opportunity. While FCX is well-placed with quality assets and strong liquidity, it faces challenges from higher Q4 costs and lower copper volumes due to an incident. BHP, on the other hand, is strategically shifting towards copper and potash, boasts strong cash flow, and offers a more attractive valuation and dividend yield.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) Sets New 52-Week High After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) reached a new 52-week high after HSBC upgraded the stock and raised its price target to $69. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.075 per share. While some insiders sold shares in December, institutional ownership remains high with major investors increasing their positions.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern unveils new double-stack route to Chicago (NSC:NYSE)
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NSC) has launched the East Edge corridor, a new double-stack intermodal route connecting Chicago and New England. This new corridor replaces a slower single-stack route, aiming to improve efficiency for freight transport. The announcement was made on Wednesday, January 21, 2026.

feed.title: 10 Biggest News Stories of the Week: Mazda3 Overpowers Ram 2500 Power Wagon
feed.summary: This article highlights the top 10 automotive news stories of the past week, led by a review of the 2026 Mazda3, praised for its powerful engine and upscale features amidst an affordability crisis. It also features the 2027 Ram 2500 Power Wagon, which now offers a diesel engine and significantly increased towing and payload capacities. The list includes a variety of other popular stories focusing on new models, pricing, and comparisons.

feed.title: Insurer Lemonade Pitches Tesla Drivers Cheaper Rates for FSD Use
feed.summary: Lemonade Inc. is introducing new automotive insurance policies for Tesla drivers that offer a 50% reduction in per-mile rates when Tesla's Full Self-Driving system is engaged. The AI-powered insurer will launch this option in Arizona this month, with plans to expand to Oregon in February. This move aims to leverage the growing interest in Tesla's autonomous driving technology.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser Co.: How a 120-Year Timber Giant Is Rebuilding the Future of Low-Carbon Construction
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser Co. is transforming its operations to become a leading platform for low-carbon construction, leveraging its extensive timberlands into an integrated system for renewable building materials and carbon solutions. The company is positioning itself as an infrastructure and platforms business, not merely a traditional paper-and-lumber company, by monetizing wood fiber and land efficiently. Through vertical integration, engineered wood products, carbon credits, and a REIT structure, Weyerhaeuser aims to address climate risk and housing shortages while providing a compelling investment for ESG-focused investors.

feed.title: Traveler Feedback Inspired This Popular Cruise Line To Go Adults-Only For Serene Sailing
feed.summary: Oceania Cruises, part of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd., has decided to transition to an adults-only experience on its vessels, effective early 2026. This decision was based on consistent traveler feedback indicating a preference for a tranquil and sophisticated environment. While existing bookings will be honored, all new reservations will be exclusively for guests aged 18 and up, aligning Oceania with other adults-only cruise lines like Viking Cruises and Virgin Voyages.

feed.title: 3 Dividend Stocks to Hold for the Next 25 Years
feed.summary: This article highlights three dividend stocks – Chevron (CVX), Lockheed Martin (LMT), and Brookfield Renewable (BEPC) – as strong candidates for long-term investment, spanning the next 25 years. It emphasizes their consistent dividend growth, robust business models, and potential for sustained returns, catering to different investor profiles from conservative to those seeking clean energy exposure.

feed.title: Prologis (PLD) Beats Revenue Expectations in Q4
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) reported its Q4 financial results, with FFO per share meeting expectations at $1.44 and revenue surpassing forecasts by $160 million, reaching $2.25 billion. The company projects future net earnings per diluted share between $3.70 and $4.00 and core FFO between $6.00 and $6.20. Despite strong revenue growth and profitability, the article notes concerns such as a moderate Altman Z-Score, insider selling, and a premium valuation.

feed.title: Barclays Raises Price Target for CarMax (KMX) Despite Underweigh
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for CarMax (KMX) from $24.00 to $28.00 while maintaining an "Underweight" rating. This decision comes amidst mixed analyst sentiments from late 2025, with other firms like Mizuho and Wedbush lowering their price targets, and RBC Capital raising theirs. Despite the varied analyst outlooks, CarMax, the largest used-vehicle retailer in the US, aims to grow its market share beyond the current estimated 3.7%.

feed.title: Carlisle Companies Inc (CSL) Shares Down 3.07% on Jan 20
feed.summary: Carlisle Companies Inc (CSL) shares dropped 3.07% on January 20th, reaching an intraday low of $348.63 before closing at $352.57. This puts the stock 19.12% below its 52-week high and 20.15% above its 52-week low. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $385.39, implying a potential upside of 9.31%, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $400.63, suggesting a 13.63% upside.

feed.title: EXPE: Barclays Raises Price Target for Expedia Group to $245
feed.summary: Barclays has maintained an "Equal-Weight" rating for Expedia Group (EXPE) but raised its price target to $245, an 11.36% increase. This follows a trend of several other analyst firms, including Morgan Stanley, B. Riley Securities, Wells Fargo, BTIG, and Bernstein, also raising their price targets for EXPE in January 2026, indicating positive sentiment towards the company's future market performance. Despite this optimism, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside of 31.3% from the current price based on its fair value calculation.

feed.title: RTX's Collins Aerospace signs three-year parts distribution agreements for C-130 wheels and brakes
feed.summary: Collins Aerospace, an RTX business, has secured three-year parts distribution agreements with Integrated Procurement Technologies, S3 AeroDefense, and Derco to enhance hardware and logistics support for C-130 Hercules wheels and brakes. This initiative aims to ensure targeted support for C-130 operators globally, improving fleet readiness for critical missions. The agreements are expected to provide exceptional value through longer service life, reduced maintenance, and increased operational efficiencies for customers.

feed.title: NYSE Content Advisory: Pre-Market Update + AI, Robotics, Autonomy In Focus at the World Economic Forum
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange provides a daily pre-market update featuring insights from the NYSE Trading Floor. Today's update highlights fractional market losses ahead of President Trump's address at the World Economic Forum and discussions on AI, robotics, and autonomy, with NYSE President Lynn Martin forecasting a "Supercycle" in IPOs. Digital asset platform BitGo is also set to list on the NYSE.

feed.title: The Travelers Companies (TRV) Maintains Quarterly Dividend of $1
feed.summary: The Travelers Companies (TRV) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $1.10 per share, reflecting a 1.63% forward yield. Despite strong financial performance and a low P/E ratio, its P/S ratio is near a five-year high, and insider selling activity has been noted. The company's robust financial health is supported by a Piotroski F-Score of 7, making it an attractive option for risk-averse investors due to its lower volatility.

feed.title: Invesco (IVZ) Upgraded to Outperform by RBC, Shares Climb
feed.summary: Invesco (IVZ) received an "Outperform" upgrade from RBC Capital Markets, with its price target increased to $35 from $25, causing its stock to rise by 1.25% pre-market. RBC cited improved organic growth and potential margin expansion by 2026/2027 as key drivers. Despite the positive outlook and strong institutional ownership, Invesco presents mixed financial signals, including declining revenue growth and insider selling activity, alongside a valuation that appears near historical highs.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on FirstEnergy to $49 From $47, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has adjusted its price target for FirstEnergy (FE) to $49, up from the previous $47, while reiterating an "Overweight" rating on the utility stock. This change indicates a positive outlook from the analyst firm on FirstEnergy's future performance. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments for FirstEnergy, including Barclays lowering its target to $50 and Wells Fargo lowering its target to $53, both while maintaining an "Overweight" rating.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares's Earnings: A Preview
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ: HBAN) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on Thursday, January 22, 2026. Analysts predict an EPS of $0.35 for the quarter, and investors are keenly awaiting both the earnings announcement and future guidance. The company's shares were trading at $17.99 as of January 20, showing a 9.44% increase over the last 52 weeks.

feed.title: essence Makeup Expands to Over 500 Walmart Stores Nationwide, Bringing Tik-Tok Viral, Affordable Beauty to Shelves This January
feed.summary: essence Makeup, a global, cruelty-free cosmetics brand, is expanding its in-store presence to over 500 Walmart locations nationwide after a successful online launch on Walmart.com in 2025. This expansion aims to make its viral, affordable beauty products, including the popular Lash Princess Mascara, more accessible to a wider consumer base. The brand will also introduce a Walmart-exclusive lip liner to celebrate the launch.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin stock hits 52-week high at $587.00
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin (LMT) stock reached a new 52-week high of $587.00, reflecting a 17.4% increase over the past year. This milestone is attributed to strong investor confidence, strategic initiatives, and a robust order pipeline, highlighted by the Mexican Air Force's acquisition of a C-130J-30 Super Hercules and an upgrade from Truist Securities to a "Buy" rating. The company's performance underscores positive sentiment regarding its future prospects and ongoing operational and financial advancements.

feed.title: DaVita Inc. Schedules 4th Quarter 2025 Investor Conference Call
feed.summary: DaVita Inc. announced it will hold its quarterly conference call on Monday, February 2, 2026, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time, to discuss its fourth-quarter results for 2025. The company plans to release the results after market close on the same day. Investors can access the webcast via DaVita's IR web page or by phone using specified dial-in numbers.

feed.title: Bernstein Maintains SLB Ltd(SLB.US) With Buy Rating, Maintains Target Price $52.3
feed.summary: Bernstein analyst Guillaume Delaby has reiterated a "Buy" rating for SLB Ltd (SLB.US) and maintained a target price of $52.3. Delaby has a strong track record, with a 69.0% success rate and an average return of 11.2% over the past year, according to TipRanks data. This rating indicates continued confidence in the company's performance.

feed.title: Why Is Prologis Stock Gaining Wednesday? - Prologis (NYSE:PLD)
feed.summary: Prologis (NYSE: PLD) stock gained on Wednesday after the industrial real estate company reported its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings, which saw increased operating income and improved earnings per share, despite revenue falling slightly short of analyst estimates. The company also reported record lease signings in 2025 and expanded power capacity for data centers, leading to a reaffirmed strong outlook for 2026 and beyond.

feed.title: IBM Q4 Earnings Loom: Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock Ahead of Results?
feed.summary: IBM is scheduled to report its Q4 2025 earnings on January 28, 2026, with sales and earnings estimates at $19.21 billion and $4.33 per share, respectively. Despite strong partnerships in AI and quantum computing, IBM's stock does not currently predict an earnings beat, holding a Zacks Rank #3 and 0.00% Earnings ESP. The company faces stiff competition, ongoing business model transition challenges, and is currently trading at a premium valuation, suggesting investors might wait for a better entry point.

feed.title: Caesars Palace Online Casino PA Promo Code BOOKIESLAUNCH: Grab $1,000 Bonus (Jan. 21)
feed.summary: Caesars Palace Online Casino PA is offering new users a welcome bonus of a 100% deposit match up to $1,000 using the promo code BOOKIESLAUNCH. Additionally, Caesars Pennsylvania Sportsbook provides a separate offer with promo code BOOKIES1000 for up to a $1,000 First Bet Bonus. The casino offers a wide range of games, fast payouts, and the Caesars Rewards program for perks.

feed.title: TD Cowen Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) Stock Price
feed.summary: TD Cowen has reduced its price target for Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) from $235.00 to $200.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 61.38%. This comes amid mixed analyst revisions, with some firms raising targets and others lowering them, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target of $194.13. The article also highlights recent insider selling activity, with the CEO and CTO divesting significant shares.
